title: coreopsis mcvaughii
intro: Coreopsis mcvaughii, also known as McVaugh's tickseed, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Coreopsis mcvaughii include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or ingested to reap its medicinal properties.
